rulings.Case Title: IN RE: GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINUM ASSOCAITON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITIONER'S HEARING ON PETITION TO INVALIDATE 2025 ELECTION AND CONDUCT CURRENT RECALL ELECTION AND WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 27, 2025, line 1. PETITIONER'S HEARING ON PETITION TO INVALIDATE 2025 ELECTION AND CONDUCT CURRENT RECALL ELECTION AND WRIT OF MANDATE. This is a real property matter per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Transferred to Department 501 for hearing on October 27, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: TRICIA YEOMANS ET AL VS. WORLD FINANCIAL GROUP INSURANCE AGENCY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 27, 2025, line 9. FINAL APPROVAL HEARING. Hearing required. The court asks the parties to be prepared to address the following issues: 1. For the majority of class members to whom notices were initially mailed to the wrong addresses, why were they provided with a 30-day opt out window instead of the 45-day window in the court's preliminary approval order? Why do the parties believe that a 30-day opt-out window was sufficient? 2. Were opt-out rates lower for the pool of class members who received a 30-day opt out window instead of a 45-day opt-out window? 3. Re the Verita error in calculating estimated payment amount: It appears from the description that Verita's error omitted a payment and therefore did not overstate any payment amount in the notices (i.e. no class member was informed the payment would be higher than the actual payment). Is this correct? 4. Please email to the clerk a proposed final approval order prior to the hearing at calendar301@sftc.org. Please be sure the final approval order includes a compliance hearing date for the settlement administrator to provide a declaration confirming that all funds were disbursed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. WILDERNESS TRAIL BIKES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 27, 2025, line 11. PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL'S Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code, sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code, section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed consent judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. MARIA S ALMANZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 27, 2025, line 12. PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA N.A.'S Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted. Plaintiff Bank of America N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant Maria S. Almanza has not responded to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Almanza. (Id., section 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Plaintiff does not seek sanctions, but Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.280, subdivision (c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction . . . on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The orders Almanza to pay $25 in sanctions to Bank of America within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: QI MING WANG VS. WEIWEI ZHANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION, TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED, AND FOR SANCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for October 27, 2025, line 7. Plaintiff's MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED, AND FOR SANCTIONS. Plaintiff Qi Ming Wang moves for a deemed-admitted order concerning Plaintiff's first set of requests for admission to defendant Weiwei Zhang. The court indicated its tentative was to grant the motion and no party opposed the tentative. On September 23, 2025, the court issued a written order vacating its tentative and notifying the parties of its sua sponte reconsideration of that tentative. The court set the matter for further hearing and authorized Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief in support of the motion for a deemed-admitted order. Plaintiff has filed no supplemental brief. On further consideration, the court sees that Zhang has not appeared in this action. Plaintiff offers no authority for the proposition that the court may enter a deemed-admitted order against a defendant who has never appeared. The motion is therefore denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
